results.title: IESA Calls Tata Electronics-ASML Tie-Up A Game Changer For India’s Chip Manufacturing Push
results.description: India’s semiconductor ambitions received a major boost as the Tata Electronics and ASML partnership announcement drew strong support from the India Electronics and Semiconductor Association (IESA), which described the collaboration as a “historic milestone” for the country’s semiconductor manufacturing ecosystem. The announcement was made during Prime Minister Narendra Modi’s visit to the Netherlands and is being viewed as a strategic step toward strengthening India’s semiconductor supply chain and manufacturing capabilities. ASML, widely regarded as the world’s leading semiconductor lithography equipment maker, plays a critical role in enabling advanced chip manufacturing used across artificial intelligence (AI), automotive, industrial, computing, and communications technologies. Tata Group, meanwhile, has been rapidly expanding its semiconductor and electronics manufacturing footprint through investments in semiconductor fabs, outsourced semiconductor assembly and test (OSAT) facilities, and electronics manufacturing. Ashok Chandak, President of IESA and SEMI India, said the partnership between the Dutch chip equipment giant and the Indian conglomerate could become a “game changer” for India’s semiconductor manufacturing ambitions. He noted that the development aligns with the recently elevated India–Netherlands Strategic Partnership roadmap, which focuses on innovation, advanced technologies, resilient semiconductor supply chains, research collaboration, and trusted high-technology ecosystems between the two nations. “India’s growing focus on design, innovation, AI, advanced manufacturing, and deep-tech partnerships is now attracting the world’s most strategic technology leaders to participate in India’s semiconductor growth story,” Chandak said. According to IESA, the collaboration further signals India’s emergence not only as a semiconductor manufacturing destination, but also as a trusted hub for semiconductor innovation, engineering talent, and future technology partnerships. The industry body also credited the support of Union Electronics and Information Technology Minister Ashwini Vaishnaw, along with policy implementation by the India Semiconductor Mission (ISM), the Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY), and multiple state governments for accelerating the country’s semiconductor roadmap. IESA additionally acknowledged the efforts of Marisa Gerards in strengthening semiconductor and technology collaboration between India and the Netherlands. The association highlighted that ASML’s engagement with India has deepened significantly following its participation at SEMICON India 2025 and subsequent leadership visits to the country, which it said have helped translate industry engagements into long-term strategic partnerships. The announcement comes at a time when India is aggressively positioning itself as a global semiconductor manufacturing and innovation hub amid increasing geopolitical focus on resilient and diversified chip supply chains. In recent years, the Indian government has introduced a series of incentives and policy measures aimed at attracting global semiconductor companies and building a domestic chip ecosystem spanning design, fabrication, packaging, and electronics manufacturing.

results.title: VD Satheesan To Be Sworn In As Kerala CM Today; 20-Member Cabinet To Take Oath
results.description: Congress leader VD Satheesan will take oath as the Chief Minister of Kerala today along with a 20-member cabinet at the Central Stadium in Thiruvananthapuram. Governor RV Arlekar will administer the oath of office. The new ministry includes leaders from Congress, IUML and allied parties, with 14 fresh faces in the cabinet. Ahead of taking charge, Satheesan announced austerity measures aimed at ending VIP culture and reducing government expenditure in the state. Senior Congress leaders including Mallikarjun Kharge, Rahul Gandhi and Priyanka Gandhi Vadra are expected to attend the ceremony.

results.description: Barring IT index, all the sectoral indices on the NSE were traded in red with with realty, consumer durables and auto shares leading the fall.

results.title: Telangana government to change holiday for Bakrid 2026
results.description: Hyderabad: The Telangana government has announced a holiday for Bakrid 2026, a festival celebrated by Muslims worldwide. As per the Telangana calendar, the government has declared the holiday on May 27. However, as the crescent moon marking the beginning of Dhul-Hijjah 1447 AH was not sighted in Hyderabad or other parts of Telangana on Sunday, ...
